metaSummary: Pokémon Champions — Regulation Set M-B en vigueur depuis le 16 juin 2026 (jusqu'au 1er septembre). Les Pokémon de la M-A restent jouables, rejoints par cinq nouvelles Méga-Évolutions : Mega Sceptile, Mega Blaziken, Mega Swampert, Mega Mawile et Mega Staraptor. Le jeu est disponible sur iOS et Android, avec la saison Ranked M-3 (17 juin – 8 juillet) et un nouveau Battle Pass. Les premiers repères de la méta M-B viennent du VR June Challenge #2 (premier gros tournoi en Reg. M-B, 174 joueurs), remporté par Louis Markl : Mega Charizard Y reste la Méga la plus jouée (29,3 %) devant Mega Floette (25,3 %), tandis que les nouveautés Mega Swampert (20,1 %) et Mega Staraptor (19,0 %) s'installent déjà dans le top. En usage général, Garchomp, Basculegion et Whimsicott dominent. Sur le circuit officiel, le NAIC 2026 (Nouvelle-Orléans, dernier événement en Reg. M-A) a été remporté par Francesco Pio Pero et la Taiwan Master Ball League (Taichung) par Chen Yu-Wei. Le Monthly Challenge Series de juin (26–29 juin, en ligne, simple, Reg. M-B) s'est conclu, remporté par Kaminchu. The Champions Arena II (en ligne, Reg. M-B, avec Sekiam, Folagor et PokeAlex) a débuté aujourd'hui : Phase 1 Swiss le 4 juillet, Phase 2 et top cut le 5 juillet — tournoi d'entraînement avant les événements live de l'été qualificatifs pour les Mondiaux 2026 à San Francisco (28–30 août) via la Victory Road to San Francisco. Play! Pokémon a également publié un nouveau récap vidéo « Top Moments » du NAIC 2026.
metaSummary_en: Pokémon Champions — Regulation Set M-B is in effect from 16 June 2026 to 1 September. All Regulation M-A Pokémon remain eligible, joined by five new Mega Evolutions: Mega Sceptile, Mega Blaziken, Mega Swampert, Mega Mawile and Mega Staraptor. The game is available on iOS and Android, with Ranked Battles Season M-3 (17 June – 8 July) and a new Battle Pass underway. The first read on the M-B meta comes from the VR June Challenge #2 (the first major in Reg. M-B, 174 players), won by Louis Markl: Mega Charizard Y remains the most-used Mega (29.3%) ahead of Mega Floette (25.3%), while newcomers Mega Swampert (20.1%) and Mega Staraptor (19.0%) are already in the top tier. In general usage, Garchomp, Basculegion and Whimsicott lead the field. On the official circuit, the 2026 NAIC (New Orleans, the last event under Reg. M-A) was won by Francesco Pio Pero and the Taiwan Master Ball League (Taichung) by Chen Yu-Wei. June's Monthly Challenge Series (26–29 June, online, single battles, Reg. M-B) wrapped up, won by Kaminchu. The Champions Arena II (online, Reg. M-B, featuring Sekiam, Folagor and PokeAlex) kicked off today: Phase 1 Swiss on 4 July, Phase 2 and top cut on 5 July — a practice event ahead of the summer live tournaments that qualify players for the 2026 World Championships in San Francisco (28–30 August) through the Victory Road to San Francisco. Play! Pokémon also released a new "Top Moments" recap video from the 2026 NAIC.

newsFeed: The Champions Arena II is underway (4–5 July 2026, online, Reg. M-B): Phase 1 Swiss started 4 July.
Play! Pokémon released a new "Top Moments" recap video from the 2026 NAIC.
Regulation Set M-B is live (16 June – 1 Sept 2026): new Megas including Sceptile, Blaziken, Swampert, Mawile and Staraptor.
First Reg. M-B usage in: at the VR June Challenge #2, Mega Charizard Y (29.3%) leads, with new Megas Swampert (20.1%) and Staraptor (19.0%) already in the top tier.
Garchomp, Basculegion and Whimsicott top general usage in early Reg. M-B play.
Louis Markl wins the VR June Challenge #2, the first big Regulation M-B event.
Monthly Challenge Series June 2026 (online, single battles, Reg. M-B) has concluded, won by Kaminchu.
Ranked Battles Season M-3 (17 June – 8 July) and a new Battle Pass are underway.
Pokémon Champions is now available on iOS and Android.
2026 World Championships set for San Francisco, 28–30 August.
